guide.description: Operational guide for agents using shadox as an execution partner.

runtime.name: shadox
runtime.boundary: Native Linux restrictions are the built-in enforcement layer, not the product's hardened security boundary.

isolation.composition_surface: ordinary command execution and shared workspace filesystem state

built_in_layer.name: native-linux
built_in_layer.guarantee: least-privilege local execution for developer workflows, not hardened multi-tenant isolation

isolation.external_boundary_role: When hardened isolation is required, the caller or deployment should supply it outside the shadox contract. Shadox remains useful inside or around that boundary because its stable interface is command execution, observability, recovery, and agent-readable state.

commands.name: check-env
commands.purpose: Detect whether the current host can enforce the requested runtime primitives.

commands.name: explain
commands.purpose: Show the effective isolation policy before executing a command.

commands.name: run
commands.output: json summary plus optional jsonl trace
commands.purpose: Run one command with isolation policy, trace capture, diagnostics, and optional versioned workspace transaction handling.

commands.name: fs
commands.purpose: Manage checkpoints, diffs, rollback, commit, replay, operation journals, and optional FUSE-backed write-level recording.

commands.name: agent-guide
commands.purpose: Print the embedded vendor-neutral agent operating guide.

commands.name: capabilities
commands.purpose: Print this embedded machine-readable capability document.

profiles.name: agent-default
profiles.purpose: Default agent profile with observability enabled and workspace write access when no explicit write allowlist is provided.

profiles.name: workspace-write
profiles.purpose: Common agent profile for writing only inside the workspace.

profiles.name: read-only
profiles.purpose: Read and execute without filesystem writes.

profiles.name: permissive-observe
profiles.purpose: Trusted diagnostics profile with Landlock disabled and telemetry enabled.

agent_rules: Use shadox run for commands that can mutate workspace state.
Treat shadox as a recovery and observability control plane; do not describe the built-in native layer as hardened isolation.
When running adversarial or multi-tenant workloads, rely on the caller's trusted isolation boundary instead of expecting shadox to adapt to a specific provider.
Read summary.json or the final run summary JSON instead of inferring transaction state from stdout.
Use rollback-on-failure for risky edits, generators, formatters, migrations, and tests that may leave partial output.
Use materialize or replay for inspection when the live workspace should not change.
Commit only when the result should become the new workspace baseline.
